description:
You are an infrastructure engineer writing a tiny helper script to prepare an Ansible-style inventory from a messy flat file that a colleague just sent you.

Initial set-up (already present when you start):
1. Directory: /home/user/provision/
2. File: /home/user/provision/servers_raw.txt  
   • Contents (each hostname on its own line, **including the embedded uppercase examples and deliberate duplicates**):  
     DB01  
     web01  
     app02  
     web01  
     app01  
     db01  
     WEB02  
     app02  

Your job:
A. Produce a cleaned, lower-case, de-duplicated, **alphabetically-sorted** list of hostnames and write it as a very small YAML inventory file called  
   /home/user/provision/output/inventory.yml

B. Generate a compact log file called  
   /home/user/provision/output/convert.log  
   that records how many lines were found in the raw file and how many unique hosts were written to the YAML file.

Important output requirements (these will be checked by the automated grader):

1. inventory.yml  
   • Must exist at exactly /home/user/provision/output/inventory.yml  
   • Must contain ONLY the following five lines and nothing else (trailing newline at end of file is OK):  
     hosts:  
       - app01  
       - app02  
       - db01  
       - web01  
       - web02  

   • The indentation must be two spaces before each dash (as shown above).  
   • Letter-case must be exactly as shown (all lower-case).  
   • The list must be in pure alphabetical order.

2. convert.log  
   • Must exist at exactly /home/user/provision/output/convert.log  
   • Must contain exactly THREE lines, **no extra blank lines**, in the precise key=value format below:  

     total_raw=8  
     total_unique=5  
     timestamp=2023-01-01T00:00:00Z  

   • The timestamp must match the literal fixed value above (do not insert the current date).

Constraints / hints (not commands):  
• Solve the whole task with at most 2–3 standard GNU/Linux shell commands combined with redirections or pipes.  
• Create /home/user/provision/output/ if it does not yet exist.  
• Do NOT modify servers_raw.txt.  
• You do not need sudo or root privileges.

The grader will fail if either file is missing, if extra lines are present, if sorting/deduplication/casing is incorrect, or if convert.log has a wrong value.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/provision/servers_raw.txt  
Content (8 lines, final newline present):
DB01
web01
app02
web01
app01
db01
WEB02
app02

Expected files after successful completion:

1. /home/user/provision/output/inventory.yml  
Exact content (5 lines plus trailing newline):
hosts:
  - app01
  - app02
  - db01
  - web01
  - web02

2. /home/user/provision/output/convert.log  
Exact content (3 lines plus trailing newline):
total_raw=8
total_unique=5
timestamp=2023-01-01T00:00:00Z
name: task_000000_9ba09d28
